name: Quintanilla de las Viñas, Peñalara, and Cubillejo Dolmen Loop
name_original: Peñalara y Dolmen de Cubillejo de Lara — circular desde Quintanilla de las Viñas
description: Hike 11.0 miles (17.7 km) to explore the Peñalara massif and the ancient Dolmen of Cubillejo de Lara from Quintanilla de las Viñas.
region_intro: Hiking around Mambrillas De Lara offers diverse landscapes, characterized by rolling hills, dense forests, and open countryside. The region features varied terrain, including paths through the Sierra de las Mamblas and routes exploring local dolmens and woodlands. These trails provide opportunities to experience the natural environment and historical sites of the area. Best hiking trails around Mambrillas De Lara The most popular hiking route is Sierra de las Mamblas — circular desde Covarrubias, a 8.9 miles (14.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 38 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the Sierra de las Mamblas. Another top favourite among local hikers is Quintanilla de las Viñas, Peñalara, and Cubillejo Dolmen Loop, a moderate 11.0 miles (17.7 km) path. This trail explores historical sites and varied natural scenery. Local hikers also love the Hortigüela, Enraizados Forest and San Cristóbal Loop, a 7.4 miles (11.9 km) trail leading through Enraizados Forest, often completed in about 3 hours 37 minutes. Hiking around Mambrillas De Lara is defined by rolling hills, ancient forests, and open agricultural land, with options for easy family friendly walks as well as more challenging hikes near Mambrillas De Lara. The routes in Mambrillas De Lara are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore Mambrillas De Lara's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Mambrillas De Lara?
A: Mambrillas De Lara offers a diverse range of hiking experiences with over 35 trails. These routes vary in difficulty, catering to different preferences, from easy strolls to more challenging treks through the region's varied landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Mambrillas De Lara?
A: The best time to hike in Mambrillas De Lara is typically during spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, and the natural scenery, with blooming wildflowers or vibrant autumn foliage, is particularly beautiful. Summer can be warm, while winter offers a different, often quieter, experience.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options in Mambrillas De Lara?
A: Yes, Mambrillas De Lara has several easy and family-friendly hiking options. There are 6 routes classified as easy, perfect for a relaxed outing with children or those preferring less strenuous walks. These trails often explore the gentler rolling hills and woodlands of the area.

Q: Are there challenging hikes available in Mambrillas De Lara?
A: For those seeking a challenge, Mambrillas De Lara offers 3 difficult routes. One such demanding trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042240105/mirador-de-las-penas-del-tuero-por-retuerta-circular-desde-covarrubias" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de las Penas del Tuero por Retuerta — circular desde Covarrubias&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 20 km and includes significant elevation changes, rewarding hikers with expansive views.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see on the trails?
A: The hiking trails in Mambrillas De Lara showcase diverse natural landscapes, including rolling hills, dense forests, and open countryside. You'll find paths winding through the Sierra de las Mamblas, ancient woodlands like Enraizados Forest, and agricultural lands, providing a rich variety of scenery.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to see while hiking?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can explore sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5455604" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen of Cubillejo de Lara&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient megalithic structure. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1042220609/quintanilla-de-las-vinas-penalara-and-cubillejo-dolmen-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quintanilla de las Viñas, Peñalara, and Cubillejo Dolmen Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great route that specifically explores these historical sites alongside natural scenery.

Q: Are there any good viewpoints in the area?
A: Hikers can enjoy several scenic viewpoints in Mambrillas De Lara. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5553367" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñas del Tuero Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic vistas of the surrounding landscape. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3568119" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Peña Alta (1078 m)&lt;/a&gt; provides elevated perspectives of the region.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Mambrillas De Lara?
A: The komoot community highly rates hiking in Mambrillas De Lara, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the opportunity to explore both natural beauty and historical sites, and the well-maintained paths that make for an enjoyable experience.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Mambrillas De Lara?
A: Many of the trails in Mambrillas De Lara are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041694646/hortigueela-enraizados-forest-and-san-cristobal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hortigüela, Enraizados Forest and San Cristóbal Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular route that takes you through a beautiful forest and past local landmarks.

Q: What should I bring for a hike in Mambrillas De Lara?
A: When hiking in Mambrillas De Lara, it's advisable to bring appropriate footwear for varied terrain, water, snacks, and sun protection. Depending on the season, layers of clothing are recommended. For longer routes, a map or GPS device is useful, though many trails are well-marked.

Q: Is Mambrillas De Lara suitable for winter hiking?
A: Winter hiking in Mambrillas De Lara is possible, offering a quieter and often picturesque experience. However, conditions can vary, with colder temperatures and potential for frost or light snow. It's essential to check weather forecasts, wear warm, waterproof clothing, and be prepared for shorter daylight hours. Some trails might be less accessible depending on snow cover.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through unique natural features?
A: Yes, some trails in Mambrillas De Lara lead through unique natural features. The region is known for its ancient forests, such as the Enraizados Forest, which you can experience on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1041694646/hortigueela-enraizados-forest-and-san-cristobal-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hortigüela, Enraizados Forest and San Cristóbal Loop&lt;/a&gt;. These areas offer a chance to connect with the local flora and fauna.
tips: Visigoth hermitage that is located very close to the town of Quintanilla de las Viñas and below Peñalara mountain.
Space where you find stones with old dinosaur footprints that had lived in these lands, which were colder in the past.

It is a funerary megalithic monument of the corridor tomb type, declared an Asset of Cultural Interest in 1985. It is approximately 5,000 years old. It is made up of a circular chamber about five meters in diameter and a wide corridor about ten meters long.
A small town in the Tierras de Lara region.
Small mountain that stands out above the rest and where at its top there is a rocky group that acts as a flag on the hill. It reaches over one thousand two hundred meters high.

faqs.question: What is the typical duration and difficulty of this hike?
faqs.answer: This circular hike is considered moderate, covering approximately 17.7 km with an elevation gain of around 330 meters. It typically takes about 4 hours and 55 minutes to complete at a steady pace. Hikers should be in good physical condition, though some sources describe it as easy with medium demand.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the hike?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Quintanilla de las Viñas. You can find parking available in the vicinity of the village, which serves as the starting point for this circular route.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied. It begins on asphalted roads before joining the GR-82 trail. You'll pass through areas with holm oaks and junipers, but also encounter stony paths with loose rocks, requiring careful footing. The Peñalara massif itself features rocky, granite compositions and impressive cliffs.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: The route is rich in historical and natural sights. You can explore the Visigothic Ermita de Quintanilla de las Viñas, the ancient Dolmen of Cubillejo de Lara, and the Church of Cubillejo de Lara. Other highlights include Dinosaur Footprints and the summit of Peña Carazo. You'll also encounter the evocative Castillo de Lara and an ancient 2,500-year-old defensive wall.

faqs.question: Are there any panoramic viewpoints on the hike?
faqs.answer: Yes, from the elevated points of the Peñalara massif, you are rewarded with impressive panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. This includes the mountain ranges of Demanda, Neila, and Urbión, as well as the entire Lara valley.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for dogs?
faqs.answer: The trail is generally suitable for dogs, but it's always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or near historical sites to ensure their safety and respect for the environment and other hikers.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the "Peñalara y Dolmen de Cubillejo de Lara — circular desde Quintanilla de las Viñas" trail.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed during most seasons. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, so an early start is advisable. Winter hiking is possible but may require appropriate gear for colder conditions and potential icy patches on higher, stony sections.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other paths. Notably, it follows parts of the GR-82 trail and intersects with the Way of Saint Olav, El Destierro, and the Camino de Covarrubias, among others.

faqs.question: Are there any technical sections or challenges on the trail?
faqs.answer: While generally moderate, the trail does have some sections with stony paths and loose rocks that require careful footing. To reach the summit of Peñalara, hikers might encounter a short, more challenging section that involves assistance from magnificently placed chains.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or accommodation near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Quintanilla de las Viñas, the starting point, is a small village. While options might be limited directly at the trailhead, you can typically find cafes, pubs, or accommodation in nearby larger towns or villages in the Burgos region for pre- or post-hike refreshments and stays.

waypoints.name: Ermita de Quintanilla de las Viñas
